jd.jd_summary: Own end-to-end AI recruitment platform delivery across React dashboards, FastAPI services, Python AI pipelines, and production RAG (retrieval + grounding) with Supabase/PostgreSQL + pgvector.
core_responsibilities: Build React dashboards for recruiters/hiring managers (filters, analytics, candidate insights).
Develop FastAPI web services for APIs, reporting, and data management.
Implement Python cron/background jobs for CV ingestion, AI scoring, and candidate report generation.
Design and operate RAG pipelines (embeddings, vector search, semantic matching, reranking, grounding).
Architect data layer using Supabase/PostgreSQL with pgvector for hybrid search.
Integrate external systems (LLM providers, workforce platforms, email delivery).
Deploy and operate services in cloud environments with basic DevOps practices.
must_haves_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in CS/Software Engineering/AI or related field.
1–4 years building production GenAI/RAG systems; ability to build complete apps end-to-end.
RAG expertise: chunking, embeddings, vector search, reranking, grounding.
DevOps/deployment: Git, REST design, testing, error handling, cron scheduling, cloud deployment.
External API integration (LLM providers, email services).

compliance_requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, AI, or related field (required).
Onsite work in Ara Damansara, Petaling Jaya.

must_have.requirement: Build production-ready Python services and AI processing jobs (clean architecture, typing, reliability).
evidence_signals: Shipped Python backend/ETL or AI pipeline code to production with ownership (design → deploy).
Demonstrates good practices: typing/pydantic models, async where appropriate, logging, retries.
Can explain tradeoffs for latency/cost/reliability in Python services or batch jobs.
negative_signals: Only notebook/demo work; no production deployment or operational responsibility.
Cannot describe error handling, observability, or data consistency practices.

must_have.requirement: Develop React.js dashboards for recruiters/hiring managers with strong UX (filters, analytics, candidate insights).
evidence_signals: Built React dashboards with complex data tables/filters and state management.
Understands UX for search, sorting, pagination, and information density.
Can integrate frontend with REST APIs and handle loading/error states well.
negative_signals: Only static UI work without data flow or real product usage.
Cannot explain component architecture or state management approach.

must_have.requirement: Implement FastAPI backend services (REST endpoints, request/response schemas, report generation, data management).
evidence_signals: Designed FastAPI endpoints with pydantic models and OpenAPI documentation.
Handles auth patterns, pagination, validation, and structured error responses.
Has implemented report generation or export flows (PDF/HTML/JSON) or similar.
negative_signals: API experience limited to simple CRUD without validation/versioning.
No understanding of async vs sync, dependency injection, or schema design.

must_have.requirement: Build real-world RAG pipelines: chunking, embeddings, retrieval, reranking, and grounded generation for candidate-job fit analysis.
evidence_signals: Has shipped a RAG system with clear retrieval strategy and grounding behavior.
Can explain chunking choices (size/overlap), evaluation, and failure modes (hallucination, missing context).
Demonstrates understanding of reranking and hybrid retrieval to improve relevance.
negative_signals: Uses the term 'RAG' but cannot describe chunking/retrieval/grounding details.
Only keyword search or prompt-only chatbot experience.

must_have.requirement: Design database + retrieval layer using Supabase/PostgreSQL and pgvector for hybrid search (keyword + semantic) and indexing.
evidence_signals: Has modeled Postgres schemas, indexes, and migrations for production workloads.
Implemented pgvector similarity queries and combined with filters/full-text for hybrid search.
Understands performance considerations: indexing choices, top-k queries, and query tuning.
negative_signals: No SQL/schema design experience; relies entirely on ORM defaults.
Mentions pgvector but cannot explain how vectors are stored/queried.

must_have.requirement: Integrate LLM APIs (Claude and/or OpenAI) for scoring and report generation with prompt engineering, rate limits, and cost controls.
evidence_signals: Implemented LLM provider API calls with retries, timeouts, and backoff for rate limits.
Uses structured prompts/outputs and can describe prompt iteration and evaluation.
Can explain token/cost monitoring and latency optimizations (batching, caching, smaller models).
negative_signals: Only used ChatGPT UI; no API integration experience.
No awareness of rate limiting, token budgeting, or safety/grounding practices.

must_have.requirement: Own end-to-end delivery across UI → API → database → AI pipeline, demonstrating product thinking and execution.
evidence_signals: Delivered a complete feature/system spanning frontend, backend, and data layer.
Can articulate requirements, tradeoffs, and milestones for shipping increments.
Shows ability to debug across layers (frontend ↔ API ↔ DB ↔ AI pipeline).
negative_signals: Only worked in a narrow silo; avoids cross-stack ownership.
Cannot describe real delivery outcomes or collaboration patterns.

must_have.requirement: DevOps & deployment fundamentals: Git workflow, cloud deployment (Render/Vercel/Railway/AWS/GCP), environment/secrets, basic monitoring.
evidence_signals: Deployed services/apps to a cloud platform with environment config and secrets handling.
Uses Git with PR-based workflow and code reviews.
Understands logs/metrics basics and rollback/incident hygiene.
negative_signals: No deployment experience beyond local development.
Poor Git hygiene (no branching strategy, no PRs, no reviews).

must_have.requirement: Scheduled/background processing: cron jobs for CV ingestion, scoring runs, and report generation with idempotency and retries.
evidence_signals: Built scheduled jobs/workers and can explain idempotency and retry strategies.
Handles backfills and failure recovery for batch processing.
Understands basic queue/scheduler patterns even if using cron.
negative_signals: Runs batch steps manually without automation.
No strategy for retries, backfills, or failure isolation.

must_have.requirement: Quality and reliability: testing strategy, robust error handling, and practical observability (logging/alerts).
evidence_signals: Can describe unit/integration tests for APIs and pipelines.
Implements structured error handling and meaningful logs.
Shows awareness of alerting/monitoring basics and post-incident learnings.
negative_signals: No testing habits; relies on manual checks only.
Ignores observability; cannot troubleshoot production issues.

nice_to_have.skill: LangChain or LlamaIndex experience building knowledge-based RAG features (document search, historical analysis).
MCP (Model Context Protocol) or AI agent frameworks experience.
CI/CD pipelines for automated testing and deployment.
Docker for containerized development and production parity.
Additional vector DB experience (Pinecone, Weaviate, or similar) beyond pgvector.

rating_scale.3: Strong; shipped in production with clear ownership
rating_scale.4: Expert; repeatable success, can mentor and set standards

calculation: Score each must-have (0–4) × weight; score each nice-to-have (0–4) × weight.
Total possible max = 4 × 100 = 400; normalize to 0–100 by (raw_score / 400) × 100.

decision_thresholds.strong_hire: >= 80 AND no critical gap in RAG or Python production experience
decision_thresholds.hire: 70–79 with manageable gaps and strong learning/ownership signals
decision_thresholds.borderline: 60–69; proceed only with clear ramp plan and urgent need

bias_guardrails: Ignore protected attributes and non-job-relevant personal information.
Do not use school prestige or brand-name employers as scoring factors absent evidence of impact.
Evaluate on demonstrated outcomes: shipped features, measurable improvements, reliability/incident learnings.
Apply consistent structured questions mapped to must-have requirements for every candidate.
Accept equivalent experience from open-source, freelance, internships, or non-traditional paths if production-like.

report_format.must_have_breakdown_item_format: id, requirement, weight, rating_0_to_4, evidence, concerns
